message:
feat(tui): add checkpoint indicator to dashboard (ntm-0fyc)

Add checkpoint status display with:
- Header badge showing checkpoint count and freshness (recent/stale/old)
- Ticker panel segment with checkpoint status
- Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut to create checkpoints
- Automatic refresh every 30 seconds

The badge color indicates freshness:
- Green: recent (<30min)
- Yellow: stale (30min-1hr)
- Gray: old (>1hr)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-0d7","title":"Implement ntm mail ack and read commands","description":"# Implement ntm mail ack and read Commands

## Purpose

Allow marking messages as read or acknowledged, which signals to senders that the message was received and processed.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Mark message as read
ntm mail read myproject 42

# Mark message as acknowledged
ntm mail ack myproject 42

# Acknowledge all urgent messages
ntm mail ack myproject --urgent

# Acknowledge all from specific agent
ntm mail ack myproject --from cc_1
```

## Semantics

- **Read**: Message was seen but not necessarily acted upon
- **Acknowledged**: Message was seen AND the recipient has taken action or committed to action

Agent Mail tracks both timestamps separately:
- read_ts: When mark_message_read was called
- ack_ts: When acknowledge_message was called

## Batch Operations

```bash
# Mark multiple messages
ntm mail read myproject 42 43 44

# Mark all unread as read
ntm mail read myproject --all

# Acknowledge all pending
ntm mail ack myproject --all
```

## Output

```
✓ Message #42 marked as read
✓ Message #42 acknowledged (ack_ts: 2025-12-10T15:35:00Z)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single message read/ack by ID
- [ ] Batch operations by ID list
- [ ] Filter-based batch: --urgent, --from, --all
- [ ] Proper error handling for already-read/acked
- [ ] JSON output mode for scripts","notes":"Already implemented with full test coverage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:49.63059-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.941768-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T21:56:13.191391-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete: newMailReadCmd() at line 311, newMailAckCmd() at line 316, with batch operations (--all, --urgent, --from, --limit flags) and runMailMark() at line 388 in mail.go","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0d7","depends_on_id":"ntm-161","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.022722-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-0fx2","title":"Task: Implement Event Log with replay","description":"# Task: Implement Event Log with Replay

Create JSONL event log for durability and crash recovery.

## File
internal/events/logger.go

## Log Format
```jsonl
{"id":"evt-1","type":"session.spawned","ts":"2026-01-03T10:00:00Z","session_id":"myproject","data":{"agents":["GreenLake","BlueDog"]}}
{"id":"evt-2","type":"agent.spawned","ts":"2026-01-03T10:00:01Z","session_id":"myproject","agent_name":"GreenLake","data":{"type":"cc","model":"opus"}}
```

## Logger Implementation
```go
type EventLogger struct {
    path   string
    file   *os.File
    mu     sync.Mutex
    enc    *json.Encoder
}

func NewEventLogger(path string) (*EventLogger, error) {
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return &EventLogger{
        path: path,
        file: f,
        enc:  json.NewEncoder(f),
    }, nil
}

// Log writes an event to the log file
func (l *EventLogger) Log(event Event) error {
    l.mu.Lock()
    defer l.mu.Unlock()
    return l.enc.Encode(event)
}

// Close flushes and closes the log file
func (l *EventLogger) Close() error {
    l.mu.Lock()
    defer l.mu.Unlock()
    return l.file.Close()
}
```

## Replay Implementation
```go
// Replay reads events from the log and sends them to a channel
func (l *EventLogger) Replay(since time.Time) (<-chan Event, error) {
    ch := make(chan Event, 100)
    
    go func() {
        defer close(ch)
        
        f, err := os.Open(l.path)
        if err != nil {
            return
        }
        defer f.Close()
        
        dec := json.NewDecoder(f)
        for {
            var event Event
            if err := dec.Decode(&event); err != nil {
                break
            }
            if event.Timestamp.After(since) {
                ch <- event
            }
        }
    }()
    
    return ch, nil
}

// Since returns all events after a timestamp
func (l *EventLogger) Since(ts time.Time) ([]Event, error) {
    var events []Event
    ch, err := l.Replay(ts)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    for e := range ch {
        events = append(events, e)
    }
    return events, nil
}
```

## Log Rotation
```go
// Rotate creates a new log file and archives the old one
func (l *EventLogger) Rotate() error {
    l.mu.Lock()
    defer l.mu.Unlock()
    
    l.file.Close()
    
    // Archive with timestamp
    archivePath := l.path + "." + time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")
    os.Rename(l.path, archivePath)
    
    // Open new file
    f, err := os.OpenFile(l.path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    l.file = f
    l.enc = json.NewEncoder(f)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Events written as JSONL
- [ ] Replay reads events from log
- [ ] Since() filters by timestamp
- [ ] Log rotation works
- [ ] Crash recovery via replay","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:32:45.156545-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T22:30:04.045923-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T22:30:04.045923-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Replay(), Since(), ReplaySession(), SinceByType(), EventCount(), and LastEvent() functions for event log crash recovery.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0fx2","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp4g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:32:55.662141-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-0kfe","title":"Implement active account optimization (first pane sets browser state)","description":"# Active Account Optimization

The key insight: first rotation sets the browser's active Google account.

## The Problem
When multiple panes hit limits simultaneously:
- Naive: Each pane does full browser auth (slow, annoying)
- Smart: First pane sets account, others follow faster

## Implementation
```go
type RotationBatch struct {
    Provider      string
    TargetAccount *AccountState
    FirstPane     string   // Does full browser auth
    OtherPanes    []string // Follow-up (faster)
}

func (o *Orchestrator) executeBatchRotation(batch *RotationBatch) error {
    flow := o.authFlows[batch.Provider]
    
    // === PHASE 1: First Pane ===
    // This sets the browser's active Google account
    log.Printf("Starting first pane rotation: %s", batch.FirstPane)
    
    o.notifyAction(fmt.Sprintf(
        "Switch browser to: %s\nThis will be used for all %d panes",
        batch.TargetAccount.Email,
        len(batch.OtherPanes)+1,
    ))
    
    if err := o.rotatePane(batch.FirstPane, flow, batch.TargetAccount, true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("first pane rotation failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    log.Printf("First pane complete. Browser account set to: %s", batch.TargetAccount.Email)
    
    // === PHASE 2: Other Panes ===
    // Browser account is now set - these go faster
    if len(batch.OtherPanes) > 0 {
        o.notifyProgress(fmt.Sprintf(
            "Rotating %d additional panes (account already active in browser)",
            len(batch.OtherPanes),
        ))
        
        // Can do these in parallel since browser state is set
        var wg sync.WaitGroup
        errors := make(chan error, len(batch.OtherPanes))
        
        for _, paneID := range batch.OtherPanes {
            wg.Add(1)
            go func(pid string) {
                defer wg.Done()
                if err := o.rotatePane(pid, flow, batch.TargetAccount, false); err != nil {
                    errors <- fmt.Errorf("pane %s: %w", pid, err)
                }
            }(paneID)
        }
        
        wg.Wait()
        close(errors)
        
        // Collect errors
        var errs []error
        for err := range errors {
            errs = append(errs, err)
        }
        if len(errs) > 0 {
            // Log but don't fail - some panes succeeded
            log.Printf("Some panes failed: %v", errs)
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}

func (o *Orchestrator) rotatePane(paneID string, flow auth.Flow, account *AccountState, isFirst bool) error {
    strategy := SelectStrategy(o.getPaneState(paneID))
    
    if strategy == StrategyRestart {
        return o.executeRestartStrategy(paneID, account.Provider, account)
    }
    
    // Re-auth strategy
    // If isFirst, full browser flow
    // If not isFirst, browser account already active - should be faster
    return o.executeReauthStrategy(paneID, flow, isFirst)
}
```

## Priority for First Pane Selection
```go
func selectFirstPane(panes []*LimitedPane) string {
    // Best candidate for first rotation:
    // 1. User-specified priority
    // 2. Near compaction (will restart anyway - ideal!)
    // 3. Least important context
    // 4. Longest idle
    
    sort.Slice(panes, func(i, j int) bool {
        // Prefer near compaction
        if panes[i].NearCompaction && !panes[j].NearCompaction {
            return true
        }
        // Otherwise prefer less context
        return panes[i].EstimatedContext < panes[j].EstimatedContext
    })
    
    return panes[0].PaneID
}
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:09.12644-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:31:40.278652-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T15:31:40.278652-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-3ogq","title":"T6: Enhanced empty states with icons and guidance","description":"# T6: Enhanced Empty States with Icons and Guidance

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Dashboard Visual Polish (ntm-af32)

## Problem Statement
Current empty states are minimal:
\`\`\`
No metrics yet
\`\`\`

This feels unfinished. Premium empty states:
- Include visual elements (icons)
- Explain WHY it's empty
- Guide users on what to do

## Solution
Create enhanced empty state component:

### Before
\`\`\`
No metrics yet
\`\`\`

### After
\`\`\`
         ◎
    No metrics yet
    
  Data will appear when
  agents start working
\`\`\`

## Visual Design

### Standard Empty State
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ◎                      │
│        No metrics yet               │
│                                     │
│     Data appears when agents        │
│        start processing             │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Empty with Action
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ○                      │
│       No command history            │
│                                     │
│    Send a prompt with 's' key       │
│     to start building history       │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Empty with External Cause
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ◇                      │
│     Beads not initialized           │
│                                     │
│      Run 'bd init' in your          │
│     project to enable tracking      │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Implementation Details

### Component API
\`\`\`go
type EmptyStateOptions struct {
    Icon        string  // Visual icon (◎, ○, ◇, etc.)
    Title       string  // Primary message
    Description string  // Explanatory text (optional)
    Action      string  // Suggested action (optional)
    Width       int     // Available width
    Centered    bool    // Center in container (default: true)
}

func RenderEmptyState(opts EmptyStateOptions) string {
    // Render centered, styled empty state
}
\`\`\`

### Icon Meanings
| Icon | Meaning | Use Case |
|------|---------|----------|
| ◎ | Waiting | Data not yet available |
| ○ | Empty | Checked, nothing found |
| ◇ | External | Needs external action |
| ✓ | Success | Empty is good (no errors) |
| ? | Unknown | Couldn't determine |

### Panel Updates
Each panel gets appropriate empty state:

| Panel | Icon | Title | Description |
|-------|------|-------|-------------|
| Metrics | ◎ | No metrics yet | Data appears when agents start |
| History | ○ | No command history | Send prompts to build history |
| Files | ○ | No recent changes | File changes will appear here |
| Beads | ◇ | Not initialized | Run 'bd init' to enable |
| CASS | ◎ | No context found | Relevant history will appear |
| Alerts | ✓ | All clear | No alerts to display |

### Files to Create/Modify
- \`internal/tui/components/state.go\` - Enhanced empty state
- \`internal/tui/dashboard/panels/*.go\` - Use new empty states

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EmptyStateOptions component created
- [ ] All panels use enhanced empty states
- [ ] Icons are appropriate for each context
- [ ] Descriptions are helpful (not generic)
- [ ] Centered layout looks balanced
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear

## Testing Notes
1. Create new session with no history
2. Verify each panel shows appropriate empty state
3. Verify empty state disappears when data arrives
4. Test at various widths for layout stability","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:59:39.740531-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T19:56:28.536716-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T19:56:28.536716-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented enhanced empty states with contextual icons (waiting/empty/external/success/unknown), multi-line centered format with title/description/action, and updated all panels. Added unit tests.","comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"ntm-3ogq","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Build-On-Existing Infrastructure Note

**EXISTING COMPONENTS** (in `internal/tui/components/state.go`):
- `RenderState(StateOptions)` - already supports Icon, Message, Hint, Width, Align
- `EmptyState(message, width)` - convenience wrapper
- `LoadingState(message, width)` - convenience wrapper
- `ErrorState(message, hint, width)` - convenience wrapper

**WHAT THIS BEAD ADDS** (incremental value):

1. **Semantic Icon Constants**: Create named constants for icon meanings:
   ```go
   const (
       IconWaiting  = "◎"  // Data not yet available
       IconEmpty    = "○"  // Checked, nothing found
       IconExternal = "◇"  // Needs external action
       IconSuccess  = "✓"  // Empty is good (no errors)
       IconUnknown  = "?"  // Couldn't determine
   )
   ```

2. **Per-Panel Empty State Guidelines**: Document which icon/message/hint to use for each panel

3. **Consider adding Description field**: Currently Hint is used for error hints - may want separate Description for explanatory text vs action hints

**APPROACH**: Extend existing `StateOptions` rather than creating new component. The foundation is solid.","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:17:26Z"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-3roo","title":"Add unified configuration validation command","description":"## Overview
Create a unified config validation command that checks all NTM configuration files.

## Location
Create `internal/cli/validate.go`

## Command
```bash
ntm config validate
ntm config validate --all      # Check all config locations
ntm config validate --fix      # Fix auto-fixable issues
ntm config validate --json     # JSON output
```

## Configuration Files to Validate
1. **Main config**: ~/.config/ntm/config.toml
2. **Profiles**: ~/.config/ntm/profiles.toml, .ntm/profiles.toml
3. **Workflows**: ~/.config/ntm/workflows.toml, .ntm/workflows.toml
4. **Context rotation**: [context_rotation] section
5. **Checkpoints**: [checkpoints] section
6. **Recipes**: ~/.config/ntm/recipes.toml, .ntm/recipes.toml

## Validation Checks

### Schema Validation
- All required fields present
- Field types correct
- Enum values valid
- Numeric ranges valid

### Reference Validation
- Profile references in workflows exist
- Recipe references in profiles exist
- Extends chains are valid (no cycles)

### Consistency Validation
- Thresholds make sense (warning < rotate)
- Timeouts are reasonable
- No conflicting settings

### Dependency Validation
- Features that depend on others are configured correctly
- Warn about unused configurations

## Output Format
```
$ ntm config validate
Validating configuration...

~/.config/ntm/config.toml
  ✓ Schema valid
  ✓ Settings consistent

~/.config/ntm/profiles.toml
  ✓ 5 profiles defined
  ✓ All extends references valid

.ntm/workflows.toml
  ⚠ Warning: Workflow 'red-green' references profile 'tester' not found in project
  ✓ 2 workflows defined

Summary:
  Files checked: 4
  Errors: 0
  Warnings: 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema validation for all config types
- [ ] Reference validation across files
- [ ] Consistency checks
- [ ] Clear error messages with locations
- [ ] JSON output mode
- [ ] Fix mode for auto-fixable issues
- [ ] Integration tests","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:59.967595437-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:59.967595437-06:00"}

### Reference Validation
- Profile references in workflows exist
- Recipe references in profiles exist
- Extends chains are valid (no cycles)

### Consistency Validation
- Thresholds make sense (warning < rotate)
- Timeouts are reasonable
- No conflicting settings

### Dependency Validation
- Features that depend on others are configured correctly
- Warn about unused configurations

## Output Format
```
$ ntm config validate
Validating configuration...

~/.config/ntm/config.toml
  ✓ Schema valid
  ✓ Settings consistent

~/.config/ntm/profiles.toml
  ✓ 5 profiles defined
  ✓ All extends references valid

.ntm/workflows.toml
  ⚠ Warning: Workflow 'red-green' references profile 'tester' not found in project
  ✓ 2 workflows defined

Summary:
  Files checked: 4
  Errors: 0
  Warnings: 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema validation for all config types
- [ ] Reference validation across files
- [ ] Consistency checks
- [ ] Clear error messages with locations
- [ ] JSON output mode
- [ ] Fix mode for auto-fixable issues
- [ ] Integration tests","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:59.967595437-06:00","updated_at":"2026-01-06T02:29:51.16659-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-3s2g","title":"Task: Implement E2E session lifecycle tests","description":"Implement end-to-end tests for full session lifecycle in internal/cli/e2e_test.go

```go
func TestFullSessionLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
    if testing.Short() { t.Skip("Skipping E2E test") }
    
    // 1. Spawn session
    session, _ := SpawnSession(ctx, SpawnOptions{
        Name:   "e2e-test",
        CC:     1,
        Safety: true,
    })
    defer session.Kill()
    
    // 2. Verify agent registered in state store
    agents, _ := stateStore.GetAgents(session.ID)
    require.Len(t, agents, 1)
    
    // 3. Verify events emitted
    events := eventLog.Since(session.CreatedAt)
    require.Contains(t, eventTypes(events), "session.spawned")
    
    // 4. Test crash recovery
    stateStore.Close()
    stateStore = state.Open(dbPath)
    recovered, _ := stateStore.GetSession(session.ID)
    require.Equal(t, "active", recovered.Status)
}
```

 {"id":"ntm-3uoq","title":"Feature: BD Daemon Mode Integration","description":"# BD Daemon Mode Integration

## The Problem
NTM requires manual `bd sync` calls to keep beads in sync. Developers must remember to run this.

## The Solution
BD has a daemon mode that NTM ignores:
```bash
bd daemon --start --auto-commit --interval 5s --health --metrics --json
```

Use Daemon Supervisor to manage it.

## Implementation (internal/bd/daemon.go)

### BD Daemon
```go
type BDDaemon struct {
    cmd       *exec.Cmd
    port      int
    isRunning bool
    autoPush  bool  // DANGEROUS: disabled by default
}

type BDDaemonOptions struct {
    AutoCommit bool
    AutoPush   bool  // Requires policy approval
    Interval   time.Duration
}

func (d *BDDaemon) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
    args := []string{"daemon", "--start", "--auto-commit", "--interval", "5s"}
    // --auto-push only if policy allows
    if d.autoPush && policyAllows("auto_push") {
        args = append(args, "--auto-push")
    }
}
```

### Policy Control
--auto-push is:
- Disabled by default
- Requires policy.yaml permission
- Requires explicit CLI flag
- Logged for audit

### Auto-Start on Spawn
```go
func spawnSession(ctx, opts) {
    if opts.BDDaemon {
        bdDaemon := bd.NewBDDaemon(bd.BDDaemonOptions{
            AutoCommit: true,
            AutoPush:   opts.AllowAutoPush && policyAllows("auto_push"),
        })
        bdDaemon.Start(ctx)
    }
}
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm beads daemon start
ntm beads daemon stop
ntm beads daemon status
ntm beads daemon health
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --bd-daemon=true  # Default
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BD daemon managed by Supervisor
- [ ] Auto-start on spawn (default enabled)
- [ ] Auto-push policy-gated
- [ ] Health monitoring
- [ ] Clean shutdown on session end","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:04.886553-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:34:19.297208-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T19:34:19.297208-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented BD daemon integration in supervisor and integrated with session monitor","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-3uoq","depends_on_id":"ntm-0c15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:17.463364-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-3uoq","depends_on_id":"ntm-bpxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:18.01467-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-3uoq","depends_on_id":"ntm-014q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:27:28.310196-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-jy1m","title":"Add checkpoint integrity verification","description":"## Overview
Verify checkpoint files are complete and uncorrupted.

## Location
Create `internal/checkpoint/integrity.go`

## Integrity Checks

### 1. File Existence
All referenced files must exist:
```go
func (c *Checkpoint) CheckFiles() error {
    for _, agent := range c.Agents {
        if agent.ScrollbackFile != "" {
            path := filepath.Join(c.Dir, agent.ScrollbackFile)
            if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
                return fmt.Errorf("missing scrollback: %s", path)
            }
        }
    }
    // Check other referenced files
    return nil
}
```

### 2. Checksum Verification
```go
type FileManifest struct {
    Files map[string]string `json:"files"` // path -> sha256
}

func (c *Checkpoint) VerifyChecksums() error {
    manifest := c.Metadata.Manifest
    for path, expectedHash := range manifest.Files {
        actualHash, err := hashFile(filepath.Join(c.Dir, path))
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        if actualHash != expectedHash {
            return fmt.Errorf("checksum mismatch: %s", path)
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

### 3. Schema Validation
```go
func (c *Checkpoint) ValidateSchema() error {
    if c.Version < 1 || c.Version > CurrentVersion {
        return fmt.Errorf("unsupported version: %d", c.Version)
    }
    if c.SessionName == "" {
        return errors.New("missing session_name")
    }
    // Validate all required fields
    return nil
}
```

### 4. Consistency Checks
```go
func (c *Checkpoint) ValidateConsistency() error {
    // Agent count matches layout
    // Profile references exist
    // Git commit exists (if git repo)
    return nil
}
```

## CLI Commands
```bash
ntm checkpoint verify <name>
# Output:
# ✓ Schema valid
# ✓ All files present
# ✓ Checksums verified
# ✓ Consistency checks passed
# Checkpoint verified successfully

ntm checkpoint verify --all
# Verifies all checkpoints, reports any issues
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File existence checks
- [ ] Checksum generation and verification
- [ ] Schema validation
- [ ] Consistency checks
- [ ] ntm checkpoint verify command
- [ ] Detailed error messages
- [ ] Tests for corruption scenarios","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:24.173347718-06:00","updated_at":"2026-01-06T02:20:27.418534-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-jy1m","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ox6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:36.789519519-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-jy1m","depends_on_id":"ntm-59ce","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:36.850148867-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-jzkc","title":"T1: Responsive help bar using RenderHelpBar component","description":"# T1: Responsive Help Bar Using RenderHelpBar Component

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Dashboard Discoverability (ntm-wb8v)

## Problem Statement
The dashboard has a custom help bar implementation that doesn't handle narrow widths:

\`\`\`go
// dashboard.go:2326 - Custom implementation
func (m Model) renderHelpBar() string {
    items := []struct{ key, desc string }{
        {"↑↓", "navigate"},
        // ... hardcoded items
    }
    // ... custom rendering (no width handling)
}
\`\`\`

Meanwhile, \`components/help.go\` has a reusable component with progressive truncation:

\`\`\`go
// Unused in dashboard!
func RenderHelpBar(opts HelpBarOptions) string {
    // Progressive truncation: removes hints from right until fits
    // Width-aware rendering
    // Consistent styling
}
\`\`\`

## Solution
Replace custom \`renderHelpBar()\` with \`components.RenderHelpBar()\`.

## Implementation Details

### Before
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) renderHelpBar() string {
    items := []struct{ key, desc string }{
        {"↑↓", "navigate"},
        {"1-9", "select"},
        {"z", "zoom"},
        // ...
    }
    // Custom rendering
}
\`\`\`

### After
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) renderHelpBar() string {
    return components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: components.DefaultDashboardHints(),
        Width: m.width - 4,  // Account for margins
    })
}
\`\`\`

### Files to Modify
- \`internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go\` - Replace renderHelpBar implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard uses \`components.RenderHelpBar()\`
- [ ] Help bar truncates gracefully at narrow widths
- [ ] Visual appearance matches existing (no regression)
- [ ] Hints hide from right-to-left as width decreases
- [ ] At very narrow widths, only most important hints shown

## Testing Notes
1. Run dashboard at various terminal widths
2. Verify hints appear/disappear smoothly
3. Verify most important hints (quit, help) remain at narrow widths

## Visual Example
\`\`\`
# Wide (100+ cols):
↑↓ navigate  1-9 select  z zoom  c context  m mail  r refresh  d diag  ? help  q quit

# Medium (80 cols):
↑↓ navigate  1-9 select  z zoom  r refresh  ? help  q quit

# Narrow (60 cols):
↑↓ nav  z zoom  ? help  q quit
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:55:44.694194-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T19:39:13.042455-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T19:39:13.042455-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced custom renderHelpBar() with components.RenderHelpBar() for width-aware progressive truncation. Updated DefaultDashboardHints() to include d(diag) and ?(help) hints.","comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"ntm-jzkc","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Build-On-Existing Infrastructure Note

**EXISTING COMPONENTS** (confirmed present):
- `components/help.go:63` - `RenderHelpBar(opts HelpBarOptions)` with progressive truncation
- `components/help.go:270` - `DefaultDashboardHints()` returns standard hints

**CURRENT PROBLEM** (confirmed):
- `dashboard/dashboard.go:2326` has custom `renderHelpBar()` that ignores the component
- No progressive truncation on narrow terminals

**APPROACH**:
Simply replace the custom implementation with component call - minimal code change, maximum benefit.","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:19:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-jzll","title":"Extend events package with unified event bus","description":"## Overview
Create a central event bus for all NTM features to communicate.

## Location
Create `internal/events/bus.go`

## Event Types
```go
type Event interface {
    Type() string
    Timestamp() time.Time
    SessionName() string
}

// Profile events
type ProfileAssignedEvent struct {
    AgentID  string
    Profile  string
    Previous string  // Empty if new
}

type ProfileSwitchedEvent struct {
    AgentID     string
    OldProfile  string
    NewProfile  string
}

// Rotation events
type ContextWarningEvent struct {
    AgentID        string
    UsagePercent   float64
    EstimatedRoom  int64
}

type RotationStartedEvent struct {
    AgentID       string
    UsagePercent  float64
    Profile       string
}

type RotationCompletedEvent struct {
    OldAgentID    string
    NewAgentID    string
    SummaryTokens int
    Success       bool
}

// Checkpoint events
type CheckpointCreatedEvent struct {
    Name       string
    Level      string
    SizeBytes  int64
    AgentCount int
}

type CheckpointRestoredEvent struct {
    Name         string
    SessionName  string
    AgentCount   int
}

// Workflow events
type WorkflowStartedEvent struct {
    Workflow string
    Agents   []string
}

type StageTransitionEvent struct {
    Workflow  string
    FromStage string
    ToStage   string
    Trigger   string
}

type WorkflowPausedEvent struct {
    Workflow string
    Reason   string
}

type WorkflowCompletedEvent struct {
    Workflow     string
    DurationSec  int
    StageCount   int
}
```

## Event Bus
```go
type EventBus struct {
    subscribers map[string][]EventHandler
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    history     *ring.Buffer  // Recent events for debugging
}

type EventHandler func(Event)

func (b *EventBus) Subscribe(eventType string, handler EventHandler) {
    b.mu.Lock()
    defer b.mu.Unlock()
    b.subscribers[eventType] = append(b.subscribers[eventType], handler)
}

func (b *EventBus) SubscribeAll(handler EventHandler) {
    b.Subscribe("*", handler)
}

func (b *EventBus) Publish(event Event) {
    b.mu.RLock()
    defer b.mu.RUnlock()
    
    // Add to history
    b.history.Add(event)
    
    // Notify specific subscribers
    for _, handler := range b.subscribers[event.Type()] {
        go handler(event)
    }
    
    // Notify wildcard subscribers
    for _, handler := range b.subscribers["*"] {
        go handler(event)
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

### Robot Mode Output
All events can be serialized for robot mode:
```go
func (b *EventBus) EnableRobotMode(w io.Writer) {
    b.SubscribeAll(func(e Event) {
        json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(e)
    })
}
```

### Dashboard Updates
Dashboard subscribes to relevant events:
```go
func (d *Dashboard) Init() {
    eventBus.Subscribe("rotation_completed", d.handleRotation)
    eventBus.Subscribe("stage_transition", d.handleStageChange)
    eventBus.Subscribe("checkpoint_created", d.handleCheckpoint)
}
```

### Alert Generation
Alert system subscribes to error events:
```go
func (a *AlertSystem) Init() {
    eventBus.Subscribe("context_warning", a.generateWarning)
    eventBus.Subscribe("rotation_failed", a.generateError)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Event interface and types
- [ ] Thread-safe event bus
- [ ] Subscribe/publish pattern
- [ ] Event history for debugging
- [ ] Robot mode JSON output
- [ ] Dashboard integration
- [ ] Alert system integration
- [ ] Tests for pub/sub","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:49:24.72355393-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:07:45.219587736-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:07:45.219587736-06:00","close_reason":"Implemented unified event bus with pub/sub, event history, and comprehensive tests"}

 {"id":"ntm-nz7a","title":"Feature: Session Coordinator Intelligence","description":"# Session Coordinator Intelligence

## The Problem
NTM ALREADY registers itself as an Agent Mail agent (the "session coordinator") but does NOTHING with it. It's just a passive identity holder.

## The Solution
Transform the coordinator from passive to active:
1. Monitor all agents in session
2. Send periodic digest summaries to human
3. Detect file conflicts and negotiate resolutions
4. Assign work based on bv scoring
5. Scale agents up/down based on queue depth
6. Handle crashed agent recovery

## Implementation (internal/coordinator/)

### Active Monitoring
```go
type SessionCoordinator struct {
    session     string
    agentName   string  // e.g., "OrangeFox"
    mailClient  *agentmail.Client
    agents      map[string]*AgentState
    events      chan CoordinatorEvent
}

func (c *SessionCoordinator) Start(ctx context.Context) {
    go c.reservationMonitor.Start(ctx)
    go c.alertMonitor.Start(ctx)
    go c.qualityMonitor.Start(ctx)
    go c.coordinationLoop(ctx)
    go c.inboxPoller(ctx)
}
```

### Digest Summaries
```go
type DigestSummary struct {
    AgentStatus   map[string]string
    WorkCompleted int
    WorkPending   int
    Conflicts     []string
    Alerts        []string
}

func (c *SessionCoordinator) SendDigestToHuman(ctx context.Context) error {
    // Send via Agent Mail to "Human" (reserved identity)
}
```

### Conflict Resolution
```go
func (c *SessionCoordinator) NegotiateConflict(ctx context.Context, conflict Conflict) error {
    // Find lowest-priority holder, request release
}
```

### Score-Based Work Assignment
```go
func (c *SessionCoordinator) AssignWork(ctx context.Context) error {
    idleAgents := c.getIdleAgents()
    triage, _ := bv.GetTriage(ctx)
    assignments := ScoreAndSelectAssignments(idleAgents, triage, config)
    // Reserve files, send assignment messages
}
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm coordinator status
ntm coordinator digest
ntm coordinator conflicts
ntm coordinator assign
ntm coordinator enable auto-assign
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coordinator monitors all agents
- [ ] Periodic digests sent to human
- [ ] Conflict detection and negotiation

 {"id":"ntm-nzxy","title":"R8: JSON Schema output command for type-safe clients","description":"# R8: JSON Schema Output Command for Type-Safe Clients

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Robot Command Polish (ntm-1pre)

## Depends On
- R6: Standardize timestamps (schema documents the standardized format)
- R7: Document null/empty semantics (schema documents required vs optional)

## Problem Statement
AI agents must learn NTM's JSON output format by:
1. Reading documentation (may be outdated)
2. Parsing example outputs (may miss edge cases)
3. Trial and error (slow, frustrating)

No programmatic way to discover the exact structure.

## Solution
Add \`--robot-schema\` command that outputs JSON Schema for any response type:

\`\`\`bash
ntm --robot-schema=status
ntm --robot-schema=send
ntm --robot-schema=spawn
ntm --robot-schema=all  # All schemas
\`\`\`

## Output Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "NTM Status Output",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["success", "timestamp", "sessions"],
  "properties": {
    "success": {
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "Whether the operation succeeded"
    },
    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",
      "description": "RFC3339 timestamp of response generation"
    },
    "sessions": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": { "$ref": "#/definitions/Session" }
    }
  },
  "definitions": {
    "Session": { ... },
    "Agent": { ... }
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation Details

### Approach Options
1. **Generate from Go types**: Use reflection to build schema
2. **Handwrite schemas**: More control, more maintenance
3. **Hybrid**: Generate base, hand-tune descriptions

Recommendation: Generate from Go types with custom descriptions via struct tags.

### Custom Struct Tags
\`\`\`go
type StatusOutput struct {
    Success   bool      \`json:"success" schema:"required,desc=Whether operation succeeded"\`
    Timestamp time.Time \`json:"timestamp" schema:"required,format=date-time"\`
    Sessions  []Session \`json:"sessions" schema:"required"\`
}
\`\`\`

### Files to Create/Modify
- \`internal/robot/schema.go\` - Schema generation
- \`internal/robot/types.go\` - Add schema tags
- \`internal/cli/root.go\` - Add --robot-schema flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] \`--robot-schema=<type>\` outputs valid JSON Schema
- [ ] Schema includes all required/optional fields
- [ ] Schema includes descriptions for each field
- [ ] Schema validates actual command output
- [ ] \`--robot-schema=all\` outputs all schemas

## Use Cases
1. **TypeScript client generation**: Use schema to generate types
2. **Validation**: Agents can validate responses against schema
3. **Documentation**: Schema serves as authoritative reference
4. **IDE support**: Schema enables autocomplete in JSON files

## Testing Notes
\`\`\`bash
# Generate schema
ntm --robot-schema=status > status-schema.json

# Validate output against schema
ntm --robot-status > status.json
ajv validate -s status-schema.json -d status.json
\`\`\`

## Future Enhancements
- OpenAPI spec generation from schemas
- Client library generation (TypeScript, Python)
- Schema versioning for backwards compatibility","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacCastle","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:58:18.251266-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.956939-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T22:00:39.486782707-06:00","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-schema flag for JSON Schema generation. Uses reflection to generate schemas from Go types. Supports all robot output types and 'all' for combined output.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nzxy","depends_on_id":"ntm-x2u3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:58:40.906588-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-nzxy","depends_on_id":"ntm-yagq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:58:40.964115-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"ntm-nzxy","author":"jemanuel","text":"## AI Agent Integration Guidance

**KEY PRINCIPLE**: Schema makes NTM output SELF-DOCUMENTING at a structural level.

**HOW AGENTS SHOULD USE THIS**:
1. On first use, fetch schema: `ntm --robot-schema=status`
2. Validate responses against schema (catch breaking changes)
3. Generate typed clients from schema

**SELF-DOCUMENTING PATTERN**:
Include schema URL/reference in every response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "$schema": "ntm://schema/status/v1",
  "_meta": {
    "schema_version": "1.0",
    "schema_url": "ntm --robot-schema=status"
  },
  "sessions": [...]
}
```

**AGENT WORKFLOW**:
```python
# One-time: fetch and cache schema
schema = json.loads(run(['ntm', '--robot-schema=status']))

# On each call: validate response
response = json.loads(run(['ntm', '--robot-status']))
jsonschema.validate(response, schema)  # Catches API changes early
```

**DISCOVERY COMMAND**:
```bash
ntm --robot-schema=all  # Returns all schemas
```

Output:
```json
{
  "schemas": {
    "status": { ... },
    "send": { ... },
    "spawn": { ... }
  },
  "version": "1.0",
  "_agent_hints": {
    "usage": "Cache schemas locally, validate responses"
  }
}
```

**WHY THIS MATTERS**: Schemas enable agents to detect API changes, generate typed clients, and self-validate without external documentation.","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:19:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-o4nx","title":"Extend config.go with account configuration schema","description":"# Config Schema Extension

Add account configuration to internal/config/config.go.

## New Types
```go
type AccountsConfig struct {
    StateFile          string           `toml:"state_file"`
    AutoRotate         bool             `toml:"auto_rotate"`
    ResetBufferMinutes int              `toml:"reset_buffer_minutes"`
    Claude             []AccountEntry   `toml:"claude"`
    Codex              []AccountEntry   `toml:"codex"`
    Gemini             []AccountEntry   `toml:"gemini"`
}

type AccountEntry struct {
    Email    string `toml:"email"`
    Alias    string `toml:"alias"`
    Priority int    `toml:"priority"`
}

type RotationConfig struct {
    Enabled            bool    `toml:"enabled"`
    PreferRestart      bool    `toml:"prefer_restart"`
    AutoOpenBrowser    bool    `toml:"auto_open_browser"`
    ContinuationPrompt string  `toml:"continuation_prompt"`
    Thresholds         RotationThresholds `toml:"thresholds"`
    Dashboard          RotationDashboard  `toml:"dashboard"`
}

type RotationThresholds struct {
    WarningPercent        int     `toml:"warning_percent"`
    CriticalPercent       int     `toml:"critical_percent"`
    RestartIfTokensAbove  float64 `toml:"restart_if_tokens_above"`
    RestartIfSessionHours int     `toml:"restart_if_session_hours"`
}
```

## Update Config struct
Add Accounts and Rotation fields to main Config.

## Update Load() and Default()
- Add defaults for new fields
- Load from TOML
- Apply env var overrides","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackHill","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:07.061843-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:34:31.053967-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T15:34:31.053967-05:00"}
